title: The EML-4 Gap as Surjectivity Tool

analysis.object: T740: The EML-4 Gap as Surjectivity Tool depth analysis

t564_eml4_gap.description: T564: EML-4 does not exist -- the hierarchy jumps from EML-3 to EML-inf

non_surjective_class_depth.description: A Hodge class with no algebraic preimage would need depth between EML-3 and EML-inf
non_surjective_class_depth.reason: It would be 'more than oscillatory but less than unresolvable'

eml4_requirement.description: Such a class would need EML-4 to exist
eml4_requirement.reason: EML-4 would be its natural depth level

contradiction.description: EML-4 does not exist (T564) therefore such a class cannot exist
contradiction.reason: If EML-4 is impossible, the gap is impossible

gap_in_argument.description: Hodge classes without preimages might live at EML-inf directly not EML-4
gap_in_argument.reason: The class is EML-3; the ABSENCE of preimage is EML-inf -- different objects

refined_application.description: EML-4 gap applies to the CLASSIFICATION of the absence, not the class itself
refined_application.reason: The classifier of 'no preimage' would need EML-4 -- and classifiers live at EML-3 (T697)

t740_result.description: EML-4 gap + d(Cat)=3: the CLASSIFIER of non-surjective classes is self-contradictory -- T740
t740_result.reason: Strong indirect argument; combined T564+T697 creates pressure against non-surjectivity

analysis.theorem: T740: The EML-4 Gap as Surjectivity Tool (S1019).

key_theorem: T740: The EML-4 Gap as Surjectivity Tool (S1019).
